commit.message:
Fix deadlock in parallel chunk downloads: sequential producer

The initial implementation launched all goroutines at once, letting them
race for semaphore slots. With 20 chunks and ahead=3, goroutine 15
could grab a slot before goroutine 3, causing head-of-line blocking:
the consumer waits for slot 3, but all semaphore slots are held by
goroutines producing results for future slots.

Fix: a sequential producer goroutine acquires semaphore slots in chunk
order before launching each fetch goroutine. This guarantees the first
`ahead` goroutines always cover the chunks the consumer needs next.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(prod): corregir descarga DXF en Vercel+Render con proxy y CORS limpio

Causa raiz: el browser bloqueaba la respuesta binaria por CORS mal
configurado, dejando el fetch en estado pendiente indefinidamente.

Cambios:
- vercel.json: rewrite /generate/* y /api/* a Render (Vercel actua como
  proxy → peticion same-origin desde el browser, sin CORS)
- vercel.json: SPA fallback /(.*) → /index.html para rutas React
- vercel.json: buildCommand y outputDirectory para monorepo correcto
- backend/main.py: CORS limpio con orígenes explícitos (no placeholder),
  allow_credentials=False, expose_headers=["Content-Disposition"]
- backend/main.py: _stream() añade buf.seek(0) y elimina
  Access-Control-Expose-Headers manual (redundante con el middleware)
- backend/__pycache__: eliminado del tracking de git (ya cubierto por
  .gitignore, pero estaban commiteados)
- frontend/.env.example: documentación correcta de VITE_API_URL para
  cada entorno; credenciales reales eliminadas del ejemplo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Support building Clawdboard from source when run from the repo

Detect if the install command is run from the clawdboard source repo
(via Package.swift) and offer to build from source using bundle.sh
instead of Homebrew. Also update uninstall to handle non-brew installs.

commit.message:
fix(web): rewrite surface showcase as click-driven tabbed interface

The scroll-triggered design had two critical bugs:
1. Dot buttons had no onClick handler — clicking did nothing
2. Six 50vh invisible scroll panels created 300vh of blank space

Replaced with a click-driven tabbed interface:
- Buttons now show icon + label and call setActiveIndex on click
- Removed all IntersectionObserver scroll-trigger logic
- Removed the 6 invisible scroll panels (eliminates blank sections)
- Component is no longer sticky — renders inline in the hero
- Tab buttons meet WCAG 44px tap targets with visible labels

commit.message:
fix: move MariaDB auth bootstrap to init-volume.sh (runs as root)

The entrypoint runs as the frappe user, which cannot use MariaDB unix
socket auth to connect as root. Moved DB bootstrapping into
init-volume.sh which runs as root before supervisord starts:
- Starts a temp MariaDB instance
- Sets root password via socket auth
- Creates root@127.0.0.1 for TCP access (how Frappe connects)
- Stops temp instance, then supervisord starts it properly

Entrypoint now uses mysqladmin ping (auth-free) for readiness check,
then verifies TCP root auth before proceeding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix social profile URLs stored with & in database (v7.9.18.30)

Root cause: save callback ran esc_url_raw() on $_POST values without
decoding HTML entities first. URLs with & in query params got stored
as & and re-saving preserved the encoding. Added html_entity_decode()
before esc_url_raw() on save so stored URLs always have clean &.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): robust scroll-reveal fallback + fix duplicate ai-skills stat

ScrollRevealInit: added scroll event listener fallback alongside the
IntersectionObserver so elements never stay stuck at opacity-0. Uses
rootMargin: 200px to fire early, plus scroll-based check for fast
scrolling edge cases. WeakSet prevents duplicate reveals.

commit.message:
docs: Add No Trace — agents clean up after completion

- KakaoTalk: Escape + Cmd+W (close window, not quit)
- Chrome: close used tabs only (never close window — other tabs preserved)
- Notion: Cmd+W + restore previous app
- All agents: remember previous app → restore after work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(controllers): add cooldown period to EquityGuardController (#67)

* feat(controllers): add cooldown period to EquityGuardController

When equity drops below min_equity_ratio, strategies are halted and
a configurable cooldown timer starts. After cooldown expires, strategies
auto-restart with current equity as the new baseline. Backward-compatible:
empty cooldown_period preserves permanent-halt behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(live): add controller config parsing to live runner

The runner was not loading the controller section from YAML config,
so EquityGuardController was never initialized in live mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(live): graceful shutdown with market order close

- Fix signal handler to call node.stop() before dispose(), ensuring
  Strategy.on_stop() is triggered on SIGTERM/SIGINT
- Switch on_stop to use Nautilus built-in close_all_positions() for
  direct market orders instead of PostLimit limit orders
- Add double-signal protection for force exit

* feat(controllers): add rolling drawdown + dual-layer protection

Layer 1: Rolling drawdown monitors equity against peak within a
configurable window (drawdown_window). Halts when current/peak drops
below drawdown_ratio, with cooldown restart.

Layer 2: Absolute guard permanently halts when current/initial drops
below min_equity_ratio (no cooldown, no restart).

Empty drawdown_window uses all-time peak (no pruning).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(live): safe on_stop with cancel-first then market close

- Cancel all open/inflight orders before closing positions to prevent
  double-fills when PostLimit has active child orders
- Use Nautilus built-in close_position() for direct market orders
- Fix backtest equity_guard config: add drawdown_window/drawdown_ratio

* fix(controllers): use strategy.resume() instead of start_strategy()

NautilusTrader's component state machine does not allow STOPPED → START
transition. Must use resume() for STOPPED → RESUMING → RUNNING.

The previous start_strategy() call silently failed with
InvalidStateTrigger('STOPPED -> START'), preventing strategies from
restarting after cooldown expired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(live): fix graceful shutdown not closing positions on docker stop

The signal handler was calling node.stop() + node.dispose() + sys.exit(0)
synchronously. But node.stop() only schedules stop_async() on the event
loop — it doesn't wait for it. So sys.exit() killed the process before
strategies could execute on_stop() → close_all_positions().

Fix: signal handler only calls node.stop() (schedules async shutdown).
node.dispose() is moved to a finally block after node.run() returns,
allowing the event loop to complete stop_async() naturally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(controllers): handle None equity on cooldown and complete dual-layer test

Move equity retrieval before strategy resume in _on_cooldown_expired,
so the controller stays halted if equity data is unavailable at cooldown
expiry instead of silently disabling all protection.

Complete test_both_layers_independent with assertions verifying that
after cooldown restart, absolute guard uses the reset baseline correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
